calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-27-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jeffrey D. Kirk of the law offices of O'Connor and Associates (415-693-9960) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Victor Meng of the law offices of Gaw|Poe LLP (415-766-7451) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FARADAY & FUTURE INC., D/B/A FARADAY FUTURE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and its alternate motion for summary adjudiaction are DENIED in their entirety. Faraday did not satisfy its summary judgment/adjudication burden that plaintiffs Domainscable, Inc. and Suraj Rajwanit cannot establish that the alleged oral and/or implied contract was exclusive. There are also triable disputes whether: 1) the parties' contract was limited to the FF.com domain, 2) plaintiffs performed under that contract; and 3) Faraday had the right under that contract to purchase the FF.com domain directly from the seller without incurring a fee to plaintiffs. Per the request of plaintiffs, the court also treats plaintiffs' unjust enrichment claim as a quasi-contract claim for restitution and determines that the claim is viable under California law. Construed strictly against Faraday, Mr. Rajwani's deposition testimony shows that he was making a distinction between a "partnership" and a "contract" and not between an "exclusive" and "non-exclusive" contract. Mr. Rajwani testified that he did not have an "exclusive partnership" because he had a "contract" and there was no reason to have a partnership and because a partnership "streches across years or months." So construed, Mr Rajwani's deposition testimony does not contradict his declaration and thus the D'Amico objections to his declaration are overruled. The triable disputes are based on plaintiffs' responses to Faraday's facts 27, 29-30 and 39-40 and on Mr. Rajwani's declaration. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No. 12355, e-mail: lgillian@sftc.org. (L&M302)
